purpose: Adds a guard in hci_cmd_sync_queue to abort command queuing when the device is being unregistered, returning -ENODEV to avoid use‑after‑free crashes.
The commit deletes an unnecessary #include <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c because linux/tty.h already provides the needed termios definitions, simplifying header usage.
Updates perf c2c documentation to include the 'N/A' store metric, adding explanatory text and adjusting the report table.

Fixes race and double‑free issues in hugetlb vma lock handling during VMA splitting and range unmapping by allocating a fresh lock for duplicated VMAs, adjusting lock acquisition/release, and making the lock a condition for PMD sharing.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply properties to the rt5682s devicetree binding YAML, documenting required regulators and updating examples.
Adds missing iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one() to release a mapping allocated during probe, fixing a resource leak.
Removes an unused work_struct field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the kTLS transmit code, cleaning up dead code.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from unsigned u8 to signed s8, allowing -1 sentinel for NULL key and removing static checker warnings.

The commit deletes two '# CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS is not set' lines from RISC‑V defconfig files to keep the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entries sorted like savedefconfig; it introduces no functional change.

The patch adds a static inline helper mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() that returns true when the device is in listen‑only or CAN‑FD mode, and replaces direct bit‑mask checks with calls to this function in FIFO and ring initialization code.

The patch modifies the RTC self‑test suite to handle the case where the RTC device node is absent. It removes the mandatory open assertion and adds a check in each test to skip execution when open fails with ENOENT, preventing spurious test failures.

The patch deletes an unused assignment to the variable 'bytes' in snd_echo_midi_output_write, leaving only 'sent = 0'. This removes a dead store that triggered a clang static‑analysis warning, without altering runtime behavior.
The patch makes rcuscale buildable without enabling RCU Tasks by conditionally compiling task‑related code and adjusting Kconfig and test configs, thus decoupling the two features.
The commit fixes a duplicated phrase in a comment of spte.h, changing “be split be split” to “be split”, correcting a typo without altering code behavior.

The patch reverts the recent logic that populated the DCN315 clock table using DCFCLK values, which caused underflow when those states were disabled. It adds a helper to map voltage to clocks and adjusts loops and asserts.
Adds the compatible string for Renesas RZ/V2L (r9a07g054-mali) to the Mali Bifrost device‑tree binding documentation and updates the enum list accordingly.
The patch renames the core struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp and updates all references across the DesignWare PCIe driver files for naming consistency.
The patch changes the printf‑style format string in xgbe‑drv.c from "%#06hx" to "%#06x" to match the promoted int type of the argument, eliminating a clang -Wformat warning.
The patch casts the upper bound constant to long in the branch‑offset range check, ensuring a signed comparison and preventing false WARN_ON triggers for negative offsets in MIPS jump_label code.

The patch removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU(), replaces it with direct calls to rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd() in the P2P power‑save handler, and deletes the corresponding enum entry, simplifying the HAL layer.
The commit adds the static qualifier to the cavium_erratum_23154_cpus array, silencing sparse warnings about an undeclared symbol by limiting its linkage to the file.

Updates devicetree binding YAML files for MSM display subsystems, removing bogus interrupt flag cells and renaming a node label to accurately reflect that the MDSS interrupt provider supplies a single cell.

The patch renames dynamic‑array fields to generic array fields, adds allocation handling for arrays in ctrl_ref, updates related logic and struct definitions, preparing the V4L2 control core to allow drivers to change array sizes.
The commit adds entries for new Qualcomm PMIC models and SoC IDs to the socinfo driver, extending the static tables that map hardware identifiers to human‑readable names.
The commit changes rtl8xxxu_print_chipinfo to compute the chip cut character directly, supporting values 0‑15 (A‑P) instead of a limited switch, thus eliminating "unknown" for valid cuts.
The commit removes the redundant is_enabled field and its related enable/disable/is_enabled functions from the ux500 PRCMU clock driver, using the core's existing enable count.

Reorganizes XIP‑related #ifdef macros in arch/riscv/mm/init.c, moving pt_ops macro next to other XIP definitions, grouping related macros, and removing redundant #ifdef blocks while adding an extern declaration.
Enables the QUP and GPI DMA nodes in the sm6350 device tree by setting their status to "okay", preparing the platform for future I2C support.
The patch updates comments and formatting in rzg2l_thermal.c: replaces a vague macro comment, corrects a typo, and moves block comments to separate lines per coding style.

The patch replaces a busy‑wait loop that could spin forever when the hardware semaphore cannot be obtained (e.g., after surprise removal) with a check that logs an error and aborts the release, thus eliminating the infinite loop.
The patch renames the driver’s master/slave mode flag to provider_mode and updates the ASoC DAI format handling to use the newer CLOCK_PROVIDER masks, adjusting related logic and error messages.
The commit deletes a conditional IS_SWAPFILE macro definition in fs/nfs/file.c because the macro is always defined elsewhere, removing dead code.

Implements kernel‑side handling of GTP Echo Request messages by generating Echo Responses for GTPv0 and GTPv1‑U, adds restart‑counter attribute and related netlink configuration, and introduces helper structures and routing logic. Enables GTP devices created via ip link to comply with the GTP spec without a userspace daemon.
The patch replaces cpumask_next* calls with cpumask_first* equivalents, adds cpumask_first_zero() implementation, and updates several drivers and core code to use the new helpers.
The patch removes the driver code that queries the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR after roaming, which always failed and spammed the console, so the driver no longer attempts to get TIM info from firmware.
Adds board configuration and ACPI match entries to support the adl_rt5682 platform, enabling the alc5682 headset codec without a speaker amplifier, following Intel BT offload design.
Adds a check in io_sendmsg_zc to reject zero‑copy sendmsg on sockets lacking ZC support, returning -EOPNOTSUPP, aligning behavior with other zerocopy operations.

Adds conditional loading of xeCJK in kerneldoc-preamble.sty by checking for xeCJK.sty and the Noto CJK font, preventing LaTeX errors on systems lacking texlive-xecjk, and updates documentation notes accordingly.
The patch adds an exception to checkpatch.pl so that macros expanding to a "case" statement are no longer flagged as errors, addressing false‑positive warnings for drivers that use case macros.

The commit rewrites the simpledrm driver to replace its simple‑KMS helpers with the generic DRM atomic helpers, introducing plane, CRTC and encoder objects and associated atomic check/update/disable functions, while keeping the driver behavior unchanged.

Adds a JPEG decode worker infrastructure to enable three decode hardware blocks to operate concurrently on MT8195. Implements hardware allocation, parameter setup, worker thread, new multithreaded m2m ops, variant registration, and supporting synchronization primitives.

The patch deletes numerous VPRINTK() debug statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb helper, as the same information is already available via tracepoints, reducing unnecessary logging and dead code in the libata driver.

Adds support for entering zstate (low‑power) during extended vblank periods obtained from FreeSync. Introduces caps, debug options, stream update flags, timing adjustments, hardware programming, and DML calculations to decide and apply the optimization.

The patch updates the hqd_destroy callback prototype in kgd_kfd_interface.h, changing the reset_type parameter from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type, aligning it with actual implementations and eliminating a CFI type mismatch that caused a kernel crash on Clang-built kernels.
The commit updates the STM32 pinctrl devicetree binding YAML: renames the property from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to enforce strict validation.
The commit adds conditional compiler flags to the ath9k Makefile to suppress a spurious -Warray-bounds warning from GCC 12, without changing driver functionality.
The patch replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss PRNG driver, removing an unnecessary pm_runtime_put_noidle call and simplifying power‑management handling.

The patch refactors io_uring fixed‑file handling: helpers now take a ctx instead of a request, introduces __io_fixed_fd_install and io_fixed_fd_remove, updates callers and moves related code to filetable files.

The commit updates the devicetree binding for NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, splitting the previous single VPU node into separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes, adds new compatible strings, deprecates the old one, and provides example DT snippets with the new power‑domain control.
The commit removes the unused IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and inlines its fields directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ, simplifying macro definitions in the iwlwifi cfg file.
